name: First Choice Healthcare Solutions, Inc.
business: First Choice Healthcare Solutions is pivoting to a national chain of medical functional health and wellness clinics offering primary care, anti-aging, hormone replacement, weight management, and compounding pharmacy services. The company aims to serve patients through a mix of insured and self-pay models, employing nurse practitioners to reduce costs. It also operates a compounding pharmacy to supply personalized medications.

exposures.sentence: We will offer our services both in the clinics, as well as via telehealth and will seek to facilitate same day and next day appointments for the client's convenience.

FCHS is committed to transforming our clients health by focusing on their unique biology and providing the medical expertise and strategies an individual will require to achieve their individual health goals.

catalysts.sentence: Our Competitive Strengths Although the healthcare services market is highly competitive, we believe the following strengths and market dynamics provide us with a competitive advantage in opening three (3) clinics by December 2026.

catalysts.event: Open thirty new clinics in the next five years
catalysts.sentence: As additional capital is available to the Company, we will pursue opening a total of thirty (30) new clinics in the next five years.

Immediately after the closing of the offering, we will use the proceeds to, among other uses, complete the 100% stock purchase acquisition of LiveWell Drugstore, a currently operating compounding pharmacy.

catalysts.event: Attain FDA 503B registration for LiveWell Drugstore
catalysts.sentence: Once the acquisition is closed, management plans to begin the process of attaining FDA approval for Live Well to become an FDA-registered 503B pharmacy, which allows it to provide services to a much larger population.

catalysts.event: Initial expansion in northeast and southwest Florida and Minnesota for 2026 and 2027
catalysts.sentence: Our initial expansion will focus on building operations in northeast and southwest Florida and Minnesota for 2026 and 2027.
